hero.title: From the Coalface to the Surface
hero.subtitle: Three generations of miners in the Glamorgan valleys

media.alt: Militia attestation form signed by David Lewis for the South Wales Borderers, Brecon & Radnor, c. 1884
media.caption: David John Lewis's oath to the South Wales Borderers — service number 1082, Brecon & Radnor battalion

media.alt: Samuel Lewis as a young man, c. 1910 — studio portrait, seated, wearing a suit with a checked tie
media.caption: Samuel Lewis, c. 1910 — age twenty-three, already below ground at Aberdare. Coal miner, son of a coal miner

media.src: media/docs/1911 census 41 Glen Road Aberdare David J Lewis household schedule 79.jpg
media.alt: 1911 census return showing Catherine Lewis's fertility columns: 12 children born, 7 died
media.caption: The 1911 return — twelve births, seven deaths, a mother's signed count

media.alt: Samuel Lewis and Elizabeth Lilian Cushen, c. 1917 — formal portrait, she standing in a white blouse, he seated in a dark suit
media.caption: Samuel and Lily Cushen — married 1917 at Merthyr. He left the coalface for the Scottish Legal Life Assurance Society and never went underground again
